body_txt: People want to have friends , to be loved , to be regarded as special , and very often all three. According to Maslow's hierarchy of needs, obtaining external affection and esteem precedes obtaining self-actualization. But even when a someone's driving motivation is to obtain love, attention, and respect from others, they might well shy away from, dismiss, forget, or outright mistreat another character who actually would be willing to provide such things. There are multiple ways this plays out. The character may feel too insecure to take the dive and become close to their counterpart, or just be oblivious to the counterpart's feelings. The character may alternatively feel contempt for them for some reason or other, or have a quantity over quality approach , preferring many superficial and distant connections to others instead of fewer and more meaningful ones. And even if a character might be motivated by external validation, their pride might not let them say so openly. In some occasions, the validatee acts picky not because they are In Love with Looks or have a Single-Target Sexuality , but as a matter of principle ; the perceived implications of only ever receiving validation from anyone but the desired person outweigh the positive feelings of getting any validation at all. For example, a Casanova Wannabe might bemoan the fact that he Only Has Same-Sex Admirers , and hesitates to reciprocate their interest because otherwise, it would mean that his desire to be wanted by the other half of the human population was a Tragic Dream all along (if they even are capable of wanting him that way ). It often appears as an example of a person feeling In Love with Love or being an Attention Whore Played for Drama . Can be a subtrope of Ungrateful Bastard or Choosy Beggar . The Ignored Enamored Underling 's boss can readily reject or ignore them in favor of someone they care about (often one-sidedly). If the character in question values the attention of a Romantic False Lead , very often it is the Previously Overlooked Paramour whose genuine interest they'll take for granted at first. Depending on the identity of the spurned party, it can also overlap with Your Approval Fills Me with Shame and Be Careful What You Wish For . This trope frequently appears in concert with True Love is Exceptional , where the validatee is destined to hook up with the validator precisely because the latter isn't their type. This is (unfortunately) Truth in Television and often associated with the "Nice Guy syndrome", but examples from real life should be avoided .
